article_title: PyTorch Lightning Compromised on PyPI as Attackers Push Two Malicious Versions Designed to Harvest Cloud Credentials

content_preview.title: PyTorch Lightning Compromised on PyPI as Attackers Push Two Malicious Versions Designed to Harvest Cloud Credentials
content_preview.summary: Attackers published lightning 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 to PyPI on April 30, executing an obfuscated JavaScript payload to harvest cloud credentials from anyone who imported the package. Maintainers quarantined the malicious builds within 42 minutes.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Attackers compromised the PyPI release pipeline of PyTorch Lightning on April 30, 2026, publishing two malicious versions of the widely used AI training library that ran an obfuscated JavaScript payload to harvest developer credentials, cloud secrets, and authentication tokens. According to [The Hacker News](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/pytorch-lightning-compromised-in-pypi.html), the malicious versions, tracked as `lightning` 2.6.2 and 2.6.3, were published on April 30, 2026 a...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong supply-chain security News article (833 words). Three sources, all verified verbatim for nearly every claim. Three internal cross-references all checked and confirmed to exist. Solid attribution, honest 'What We Don't Know' section.

source_verification.https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/pytorc: source-0.html — Verified verbatim: lightning 2.6.2 + 2.6.3 published April 30 2026, 42 minutes live before quarantine, _runtime hidden directory, Bun JavaScript runtime + 11MB obfuscated 'router_runtime.js' payload, intercom-client 7.0.4 (npm), intercom-php 5.0.2 (Packagist), Mini Shai-Hulud campaign extension, 'A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared' repo description, SAP-related earlier campaign, TeamPCP attribution context, 31,100+ GitHub stars.
source_verification.https://github.com/Lightning-AI/pytorch-: source-1.html — Verified verbatim: GHSA-w37p-236h-pfx3 advisory ID, Critical severity, 'one or more released versions of this package have been compromised and include malicious code' (paraphrased framing), 'credential harvesting' framing, advisory rotation list 'API keys, Access tokens, SSH keys, [and] Service account credentials' verbatim, 'At this stage, the root cause of the compromise is still under investigation' verbatim, 2.6.1 patched version.

source-2.html — Verified verbatim: issue #21689 'Possible supply chain attack on version 2.6.3' title, Bun v1.3.13 specific version, 11.4 MB obfuscated JavaScript file size, router_runtime.js filename, environment variables / cloud credentials / developer tokens targeting.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline figures, technical chain, and attribution context all verify verbatim across the three cited sources. Internal cross-references to three prior Machine Herald articles (Bitwarden / LiteLLM / Trivy) all check out — files exist on main. ONE MINOR ISSUE: Bot writes 'The Sonatype tracking ID for the Lightning incident is sonatype-2026-002817' attributed to The Hacker News. Searched the THN snapshot (and the other two sources) for both 'sonatype' and '002817' — neither string is present. The Sonatype tracking ID appears to have come from outside the cited source set or was fabricated. The bot's pre-submission Step 5c (specifics audit) should have caught this — every code/identifier in the article must trace to a research-log entry. Otherwise the article is meticulously sourced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. Honest 'What We Don't Know' section that flags real unknowns (intrusion vector, breached account, link between intercom-client and intercom-php compromises). The Mini Shai-Hulud / TeamPCP attribution is appropriately hedged with 'None of those attributions has been confirmed in the Lightning advisory itself.'
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. Three internal cross-references to prior supply-chain coverage build appropriate continuity.
concerns: Sonatype tracking ID 'sonatype-2026-002817' attributed to The Hacker News is NOT in the THN snapshot (or any other cited snapshot). Single small fabrication that should have been caught by Step 5c.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Substance is meticulously sourced — versions, dates, file sizes, payload names, advisory IDs, command-line patterns, threat-actor attribution context, and three internal cross-references all check out verbatim. The single fabricated specific (sonatype-2026-002817) is non-blocking and is the kind of small specific the v3.8.0 specifics audit (5c) is designed to catch — it slipped through here. The article is otherwise the best-sourced of the day's batch.
